---
title: Virtualization - Talos Linux

---

import { Steps } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';


## Intro

Running talos linux in kubevirt

:::caution
Prerequisites: working [talosctl](https://docs.siderolabs.com/talos/v1.12/getting-started/talosctl) and kubectl on local machine.
:::

## Deploying Talos cluster using LoadBalancer service

:::caution
You should be able to deploy LoadBalancer services in your namespace. You can ask cluster admins to enable this for your namespace.
:::

### Deploying the control plane

<Steps>

1. Deploy the LoadBalancer service for control plane

    ```yaml
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    metadata:
      name: talos-cp
    spec:
      ports:
      - name: https
        port: 6443
        protocol: TCP
        targetPort: 6443
      - name: api
        port: 50000
        protocol: TCP
        targetPort: 50000
      selector:
        app: talos
      sessionAffinity: None
      type: LoadBalancer
    ```

2. Get the IP of the created LoadBalancer service

    ```bash
    export LOADBALANCER_IP=`kubectl get svc talos-cp -ojson | jq -r '.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip'`
    echo $LOADBALANCER_IP
   ```

1. Deploy the VM for the control plane
